c--c-data-structures--draft.title: Data Structures in C
c--c-data-structures--draft.description: Build and analyze recursive algorithms, hash-based pair detection, balanced search trees, stack-backed priority queues, and pointer-based heaps in a multi-file C workspace.
c--c-data-structures--draft.moreComingSoon: Later modules can add linked structures, graph traversal, sorting, and deeper asymptotic analysis.

c--c-data-structures--draft-module-1-alg.title: Algorithm Design Lab
c--c-data-structures--draft-module-1-alg.description: Turn five written algorithm-design problems into executable C experiments with visual traces, correctness checks, and runtime analysis.
outcomes: Connect a recursive call tree to a recurrence and a tight asymptotic bound.
Use bounded pair sums to detect equal-sum disjoint pairs in quadratic time.
Build and rebuild almost-balanced binary search trees in linear time.
Implement a priority queue using only legal stack operations.
Insert into a pointer-based complete heap by following the binary path of the next index.
why: Executable experiments make written runtime arguments concrete without replacing the required reasoning.
The same C profile, file workspace, compiler, and grader can support later systems and data-structures courses.

c--c-data-structures--draft-algorithm-de.title: From Written Algorithms to Working C
c--c-data-structures--draft-algorithm-de.description: Study each algorithm with a step animation, then implement and verify it in a focused multi-file C project.
bullets: O(log n) insertion in a pointer-based heap
